title: extracted_claim_fields.json -- core claim fact card v0.2
description: Structured core facts extracted from claim documents. Produced by claim-analysis's checkpoint 1 (Claim Field Extraction). Field structure is fixed to three shapes -- single-value (value_field), date (date_field), period (period_field). No other shape is permitted. v0.2 (from CASE_021's first full end-to-end run): added the named fields that run actually produced but had no slot for (imaging_date, diagnosis_date, claim_received_date, policy_contract_date, claim_item, disposition, insurers -- they ended up smuggled through `warnings`, losing their typed structure and evidence links); and additionalProperties switched oneOf -> anyOf because a YYYY-MM-DD string satisfies both value_field and date_field, so oneOf's exactly-one rule made every ad-hoc date field fail validation.

additionalProperties.description: New fields also use one of the three fixed shapes. anyOf, not oneOf: a YYYY-MM-DD string is a valid value_field AND a valid date_field, so exactly-one matching is impossible for ad-hoc date fields -- at-least-one is the correct constraint.

is_primary.description: For fields where multiple documents disagree (e.g. diagnosis_name/kcd_code): whether this is the primary (headline) value per the document-character-based priority rule in claim-analysis.md, as opposed to a secondary value kept alongside it. Absent when there's no disagreement to label.

value_field.description: Single-value shape: diagnosis name, KCD code, surgery name, hospital name, etc.

normalized_value.description: Normalized value, if applicable. e.g. lumbar_disc_herniation

date_field.description: Date shape: accident date, onset date, etc. value is YYYY-MM-DD.

period_field.description: Period shape: treatment period, admission period, etc.

days.description: Inclusive day count. null if end_date is unknown.
